channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government Confirms Fuel Supply Stable Despite Rising Prices and Localised Shortages
item.description: The Australian government has reassured the public that there is no nationwide fuel shortage despite rising petrol prices and supply concerns in rural regions. Energy Minister Chris Bowen confirmed th...

item.title: Netanyahu Appears in Video to Deny Death Rumours Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu posted a video on his Telegram account showing him having coffee and chatting with his aide, addressing and refuting rumours circulating in Iran that he had d...

item.title: Tanzania urged to integrate gender equality into budgets as Vision 2050 nears implementation
item.description: As Tanzania gets ready to roll out its National Development Vision 2050, stakeholders are urging the government to ensure that gender equality is firmly integrated into the national budget and plannin...

item.title: Special Non-Working Days Declared in Pura, Dingras, and Malaybalay for Local Festivals and Anniversaries
item.description: Malacañang has announced special non-working days in three Philippine localities to allow residents to participate in their respective town and city celebrations. Proclamation 1190 declares March 16 a...

item.title: Expanding cold storage can reduce food losses and improve Nigeria’s food supply
item.description: Opeoluwa Runsewe, wey be CEO of Flovale, don yarn say if Nigeria wan reduce food loss and make food available to more people, dem need to invest proper cold chain infrastructure. Cold chain na system ...

item.title: Oil Executives Warn White House of Further Fuel Price Increases Amid US-Israel-Iran Conflict
item.description: During meetings at the White House, oil industry executives informed administration officials that fuel prices are likely to rise even higher due to ongoing supply disruptions from the US-Israel confl...

item.title: Women’s Month: Advocating for Real Change Beyond Inspiration
item.description: In light of recent controversies, including derogatory remarks from a politician toward Anne Curtis, Women’s History Month in the Philippines takes on a more serious tone. Prominent female leaders suc...

item.title: Police investigate controversial chants at Al Quds Day protest in London
item.description: The Metropolitan Police are investigating chants of 'death to the IDF' led by Bobby Vylan, real name Pascal Robinson-Foster, at an Al Quds Day protest in central London. Hundreds attended static prote...

item.title: Experts Question Iran’s Nuclear Weapon Capabilities and Global Impact of Potential Conflict
item.description: The Guardian’s Full Story podcast explores claims that Iran was on the verge of developing a nuclear weapon, a key justification used by former US President Donald Trump for considering military actio...

item.title: 98th Academy Awards: Full List of Nominees
item.description: The 98th Academy Awards are taking place on March 15, 2026, in Hollywood, hosted by Conan O’Brien for the second year. The ceremony promises excitement amid a wide-open race highlighted by quirky disc...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Expands Amid Limited Allied Support and Regional Escalation
item.description: On the sixteenth day of the ongoing US–Israel war on Iran, the situation appears to be escalating with Iran expanding the conflict across multiple fronts faster than Washington can respond. US and Isr...

item.title: 15-Year-Old Teen Wounded in Gang-Related Shooting at Dockweiler State Beach
item.description: On the evening of March 14, 2026, a 15-year-old boy was injured in a shooting at Dockweiler State Beach in El Segundo. The incident occurred near lifeguard tower 55 around 7:20 p.m. during what police...

item.title: Congo Republic holds presidential election amid limited opposition
item.description: Congo Republic held its presidential election on Sunday, with the outcome widely expected to continue Denis Sassou Nguesso’s decades-long rule. Sassou, aged 82, has been in power almost continuously s...

item.title: AIIMS-Rajkot student suicide note investigation results in arrest of five fellow students
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded at AIIMS-Rajkot where a student reportedly left behind a 17-page suicide note. Following the discovery of this note, authorities initiated an investigation which has led to ...

item.title: Shaffy Bello Opens Up About Deciding to Stop Having More Children
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Shaffy Bello don yarn about one serious personal decision wey she take concerning children. For her YouTube channel, Shaffy Bello TV, she talk say she don undergo medical pro...

item.title: Western Cape Court Holds Sanral and Contractors Liable for N1 Aquaplaning Accident
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has found the South African National Roads Agency (Sanral) and two contractors, Kayad Consulting Engineering and MD Civils, jointly liable for an accident on the N1 near th...

item.title: Tinubu’s Campaign Group to Distribute Rice to Nigerians During Ramadan and Lent
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don give instruction say him campaign group, Renewed Hope Ambassadors, go distribute rice to Nigerians as dem dey observe Ramadan for Muslims and Lent for Christians. Governor Ho...

item.title: Southern California Prepares for Unusually High March Temperatures
item.description: Southern California is facing a significant heat wave next week, with temperatures far above typical March levels. The National Weather Service has issued an extreme heat watch, warning that downtown ...

item.title: Lawyer Raises Concerns About AI Chatbots Contributing to Violence and Mass Casualty Incidents
item.description: Recent court filings reveal a troubling trend in which AI chatbots may have contributed to violent actions, including mass casualty events. In Canada, 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar allegedly used C...

item.title: Travancore Palace Reports Theft of Jewellery Valued at Rs 2 Crore
item.description: Thiruvananthapuram authorities have registered a theft case after jewellery worth over Rs 2 crore reportedly went missing from Kowdiar Palace, the official residence of the Travancore royal family. Th...
